item.title: Regeneron Gene Therapy Approval (Otarmeni)
item.description:
• Summary: The FDA approved Regeneron's gene therapy Otarmeni for a form of congenital hearing loss, marking a breakthrough in genetic medicine. This approval represents a new era for treating hereditary deafness using a one-time gene therapy designed to restore hearing by delivering a functional copy of the affected gene directly into the inner ear. The decision was accompanied by a priority review voucher. Regeneron's therapy addresses an unmet need in rare disease, offering the first approved treatment for this specific genetic hearing loss condition.
  • MoAs: Gene therapy: Delivers a functional copy of the gene responsible for hearing loss via adeno-associated virus (AAV) vector to restore auditory function.
  • Sources: FiercePharma, BioPharma Dive, Endpoints News, BioSpace

item.title: Heart Cancer Rarity Study
item.description:
• Summary: A new study proposes a theory explaining why heart cancer (primary cardiac tumors) is extremely rare, potentially due to rapid cell turnover or enhanced immune surveillance in cardiac tissue.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News
